text: Phase 1 clinical trials, 2000–2025. Phase 1 is where a new drug first reaches people. Adjusting for population — trials per 100,000 residents — lets us compare a big country and a small one: the United States and Australia.
Scroll to move through time. Each line traces out year by year. The U.S. starts far ahead, but Australia climbs steeply — and pulls in front per capita after the mid-2010s.
